full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Implementing the arc-cosine kernel functions from the paper's equations
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Implement the arc-cosine kernel (Equations 3-7, 12-13) from 'Kernel Methods for Deep Learning' so it can be used as a precomputed kernel in sklearn SVC. Need: J_0, J_1, J_2 angular functions, single-layer kernel, and multilayer recursive kernel. Include sanity checks that k_0(x,x)=1 and k_1(x,x)=||x||^2.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Generated the kernel implementation with proper vectorized NumPy operations, numerical safeguards (clipping cos_theta to [-1,1]), and zero-norm handling. The sanity checks confirmed correct implementation.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Added the zero-norm guard (np.where(norms_outer > 0, ...)) which the initial LLM output did not include — this was needed because some digits images have all-zero pixels after normalization. Also modified the multilayer kernel to follow the paper's finding of using n=1 at layers > 1, which the LLM initially did not implement.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified k_0(x,x)=1 and k_1(x,x)=||x||^2 numerically. Hand-computed J_0(0)=pi, J_0(pi)=0, J_1(pi/2)=1 to sanity-check the angular functions. Confirmed kernel matrices are symmetric and positive semi-definite.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Setting up the SVM training pipeline with validation-based hyperparameter tuning
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Write a function that trains an SVM with a precomputed kernel, tunes C on a validation set from [0.1, 1, 10, 100], then retrains on train+val and evaluates on test. This mirrors the paper's Section 2.4 methodology.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided the train_and_evaluate_svm function with the correct workflow: compute kernel matrices, grid search C on validation, retrain on combined train+val, and evaluate on test.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Changed the C grid from the LLM's suggested [0.01, 0.1, 1, 10, 100, 1000] to [0.1, 1, 10, 100] to keep computation faster on the toy dataset. Also added error rate reporting alongside accuracy to match the paper's convention.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified that the pipeline correctly retrains on train+val before test evaluation, matching the paper's stated methodology in Section 2.4.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Structuring the three experiments (single-layer, multilayer, mixed-degree)
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me structure three experiments: (1) single-layer kernels n=0,1,2, (2) multilayer n=1 at depths 1-4, (3) mixed-degree n=0 at layer 1 + n=1 at deeper layers. Also add an RBF baseline with tuned gamma.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Organized the experiments clearly with named loops and result dictionaries, and added the RBF baseline with both C and gamma tuning.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Added the RBF gamma search over ['scale', 0.01, 0.1] which the LLM only searched over numeric values. Also changed variable names to be more descriptive (e.g., degree_names dictionary).
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Confirmed the mixed-degree setup matches Figure 2-3 caption in the paper. Verified RBF baseline runs correctly with tuned parameters.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Implement the arc-cosine kernel (Equations 3-7, 12-13) for use as a precomputed kernel in sklearn SVC.
top_5_prompts.why_important: This was the core implementation prompt — the entire reproduction depends on getting the kernel formula right.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Write a function that trains SVM with precomputed kernel, tunes C on validation, retrains on combined data.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Established the evaluation pipeline used across all three experiments and the ablation study.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Structure three experiments: single-layer, multilayer, mixed-degree, plus RBF baseline.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Organized the experimental structure that produces the results compared against the paper.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Task 2.2 of Part B.
